name: QTI 3 Repository Projection Dictionary
plain_description: A structured dictionary for the platform's QTI repository model. It describes the QTI package, item, test, interaction, scoring, accessibility, alignment, and artifact fields the platform exposes as SQL and JSON. Full QTI XML remains preserved as the source artifact.
model_scope: Repository, import, search, metadata, alignment, and governance. This is not an assessment delivery/runtime conformance claim.

objects.name: QTI Package
objects.plain_description: An imported assessment bundle containing QTI XML, resources, media, metadata, and a manifest.
objects.why_it_exists: Apps and AI agents need one record that says where the package came from, whether it is valid, and where the original artifact is stored.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this QTI package.
The package ID supplied by the publisher, item bank, authoring tool, or import process.
The QTI version the package claims to use.
The publisher, item bank, LMS, or authoring tool that supplied the package.
Whether the platform has checked the package and found it valid, invalid, warning-only, or not yet checked.
How many validation problems were found during import.
The location of the package manifest file inside the package.
Where the platform stores the original uploaded package file.
When the platform received or imported the package.

objects.name: QTI Assessment Item
objects.plain_description: One question, task, or prompt plus its response variables, scoring rules, body content, feedback, and accessibility metadata.
objects.why_it_exists: Item banks, search, reuse, alignment, accessibility review, and item-level analytics need item records.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this item.
The QTI package this item came from.
The local QTI identifier for the item inside its package.
The item title used to browse or select the item.
An authoring-system label for the item.
The main language of the item as a language tag.
The tool that created the item.
The version of the authoring tool that created the item.
Whether the item can change based on learner responses or test logic.
Whether timing can affect response processing or scoring for this item.
A safe summary of the visible question body, not necessarily the full item HTML/XML.
fields.school_example: Choose all models that show one half.

fields.plain_description: The main way the learner answers this item.
The highest normal score this item can produce when known.
Whether the item includes a declared correct response.
The accessibility catalog record attached to this item, if any.
The package-relative XML file or resource path for this item.

objects.name: QTI Assessment Stimulus
objects.plain_description: Shared content such as a reading passage, chart, scenario, or media asset used by one or more items.
objects.why_it_exists: Stimuli need search, reuse, accessibility review, and many-to-many links to items.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this stimulus.
The QTI package this stimulus came from.
The local QTI identifier for the stimulus.
The stimulus title used to browse or select it.
The main language of the stimulus as a language tag.
A safe summary of the passage, chart, media, or other shared content.
fields.school_example: A short passage explaining evaporation and condensation.

fields.plain_description: The accessibility catalog record attached to this stimulus, if any.
The package-relative XML file or resource path for this stimulus.

objects.name: QTI Assessment Test
objects.plain_description: A whole test definition, including parts, sections, item references, timing, outcomes, and feedback.
objects.why_it_exists: Schools and apps need to search, assign, inspect, and align whole assessments, not only individual items.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this test.
The QTI package this test came from.
The local QTI identifier for the test.
The test title people see.
Style or category tokens from the QTI test; this is not a school class.
The tool that created the test.
The version of the tool that created the test.
The maximum time allowed for the test when known, in seconds.
A safe summary of how test-level outcomes are calculated.
fields.school_example: Sum item scores into SCORE and compare with mastery threshold.

fields.plain_description: The package-relative XML file or resource path for this test.

objects.name: QTI Test Part
objects.plain_description: A major part of a test that controls navigation, submission, timing, and item-session behavior.
objects.why_it_exists: Delivery and analytics need to know how learners are allowed to move through a test.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this test part.
The test that contains this part.
The local QTI identifier for this test part.
The title for this part, if shown or managed separately.
Whether the learner must go in order or can move around.
Whether answers are submitted item-by-item or together.
The maximum time allowed for this part, in seconds.
The timing/review/attempt-control record for items in this part, if modeled separately.

objects.name: QTI Assessment Section
objects.plain_description: A group inside a test that can organize item references, nested sections, randomization, and section-level rules.
objects.why_it_exists: Sections are needed for test construction, search, item selection, and analytics by part of a test.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this section.
The test that contains this section.
The section that contains this section, if sections are nested.
The local QTI identifier for the section.
The title used to browse or present the section.
Whether this section must be selected when test selection rules run.
Whether this section keeps its position when nearby content is shuffled.
Whether the learner can see this as a visible section.
Whether children of an invisible shuffled section should move together as a group.
How many child parts should be selected from this section, if selection is used.
Whether child parts may be presented in shuffled order.

objects.name: QTI Item Reference
objects.plain_description: A test's reference to an item file, including test-specific category, timing, weight, and selection behavior.
objects.why_it_exists: The same item can appear in more than one test or section with different rules.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this item reference.
The section containing this item reference.
The resolved platform item record, if the href has been matched to an item.
The test-local identifier for this item reference.
The package-relative link to the item XML file.
Whether this item must be selected when selection rules run.
Whether this item keeps its position during shuffling.
Category tags used for reporting, subscores, or selection rules.
Test-specific score weight for this item.
The maximum time allowed for this item reference, in seconds.

objects.name: QTI Variable Declaration
objects.plain_description: A response, outcome, template, or context variable used by an item or test.
objects.why_it_exists: Variables explain how answers, scores, status values, templates, and context values are named and typed.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this variable declaration.
The kind of QTI object that owns this variable.
The platform ID of the item, test, or other owner object.
Whether this variable stores an answer, score/status outcome, template value, or context value.
The QTI variable name.
Whether the variable holds one value, many values, ordered values, or a record.
The type of each value held by the variable.
A safe summary of the default value, if any.
A safe summary of the correct response, if the item declares one.
A safe summary of how response values map to scores or outcomes.
The audience this outcome or feedback-related variable is intended for.

objects.name: QTI Interaction
objects.plain_description: The part of an item where the learner answers, such as choosing, typing, matching, uploading, clicking, or using media.
objects.why_it_exists: Apps need interaction-level search, accessibility review, analytics, and delivery planning.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this interaction.
The item containing this interaction.
The response variable that stores the learner's answer for this interaction.
The QTI interaction family.
A safe summary of the instructions or prompt shown with the interaction.
Whether choices should appear in randomized order.
The smallest number of choices a learner must select.
The largest number of choices a learner may select; zero often means no limit.
The smallest number of pairings or matches required.
The largest number of pairings or matches allowed.
Whether layout naturally reads horizontally or vertically.
Expected response length for text interactions.
Whether typed text is plain, preformatted, or XHTML.
The hotspot shape used by graphic interactions.

objects.name: QTI Processing Rule
objects.plain_description: A preserved summary of response, outcome, template, branch, or precondition logic from QTI XML.
objects.why_it_exists: The platform needs searchable processing metadata without pretending to reimplement the full QTI runtime.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this processing rule.
The kind of QTI object that owns this rule.
The platform ID of the object that owns this rule.
The kind of processing rule.
A reusable QTI processing template URI, if one is referenced.
The target item, section, outcome, or special destination used by this rule.
A safe plain-language summary of the rule logic.
fields.school_example: If SCORE is below 1, show remediation feedback.

objects.name: QTI Feedback
objects.plain_description: Feedback, rubric, or instructional content controlled by outcomes, timing, or audience.
objects.why_it_exists: Feedback and rubrics are important for delivery, review, accessibility, and analytics.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this feedback record.
The kind of QTI object that owns this feedback.
The platform ID of the object that owns this feedback.
Whether this is item feedback, test feedback, or a rubric block.
The outcome variable that controls whether this feedback appears.
Whether matching the identifier shows or hides the feedback.
The value tested against an outcome to decide whether the feedback applies.
An optional title for the feedback or rubric.
Whether test feedback appears during the test or at the end.
The purpose of a rubric block.
The audience that should see this feedback or rubric.
A safe summary of the feedback or rubric content.
fields.school_example: Explains why multiplying numerator and denominator by the same number keeps value equal.

objects.name: QTI Accessibility Support
objects.plain_description: Alternative content, catalog entries, and accessibility support metadata attached to QTI content.
objects.why_it_exists: Assessment content must expose accessibility supports without leaking learner accommodations unnecessarily.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this accessibility support record.
The kind of QTI object this support belongs to.
The platform ID of the object this support belongs to.
The kind of accessibility support available.
The QTI catalog ID reference for this support.
Whether text-to-speech should be suppressed for this content.
A safe summary of the alternative or support content.
Whether this support metadata may reveal accessibility needs or accommodations.

objects.name: QTI Companion Material
objects.plain_description: A calculator, ruler, protractor, file, physical material, or vendor extension allowed or required during an item.
objects.why_it_exists: Assessment delivery, accommodations, and proctoring need to know which materials are permitted.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this companion material.
The item this companion material applies to.
The kind of companion material.
The calculator capability allowed or required.
A human-facing name for the material.
Whether the item requires this material to be available.
A package file path, external URI, or local reference for the material.

objects.name: QTI Alignment
objects.plain_description: A link from QTI content to an academic standard, competency, skill, or other alignment target.
objects.why_it_exists: The platform uses CASE as the canonical standards graph, but it preserves QTI source alignment metadata.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this alignment.
The kind of QTI object being aligned.
The platform ID of the aligned QTI object.
What kind of target the QTI content is aligned to.
The CASE item ID, URI, or local standard identifier.
The human-readable standard, competency, or skill label.
Where the original QTI or package metadata described this alignment.

objects.name: QTI Package Artifact
objects.plain_description: A file preserved from the QTI package, such as XML, media, stylesheet, manifest, or other referenced resource.
objects.why_it_exists: The platform must preserve the original files while projecting only useful search and governance fields into tables.

fields.plain_description: The platform's stable ID for this package artifact.
The package containing this artifact.
The package-relative path to the file.
The file's MIME type when known.
The role this file plays in the QTI package.
A hash used to confirm the file did not change.
Where the platform stores this artifact after import.

qti_version.label: QTI 3.0
qti_version.plain_description: The current QTI 3 assessment, section, and item model targeted by the platform.

qti_version.label: QTI 2.2
qti_version.plain_description: A legacy QTI version the platform may preserve or migrate, but not the lead model.

validation_status.label: Pending
validation_status.plain_description: The platform has not checked the package yet.

validation_status.label: Invalid
validation_status.plain_description: The package failed validation and should not be trusted for delivery.

validation_status.label: Warning
validation_status.plain_description: The package is usable but has non-blocking issues that should be reviewed.

true_false.label: False
true_false.plain_description: No, this condition does not apply.

owner_object_type.label: Test part
owner_object_type.plain_description: A major part of a QTI test.

owner_object_type.label: Item reference
owner_object_type.plain_description: A test or section reference to an item.

owner_object_type.label: Interaction
owner_object_type.plain_description: The answer interaction inside an item.

declaration_kind.label: Response
declaration_kind.plain_description: A variable that stores a learner answer.

declaration_kind.label: Outcome
declaration_kind.plain_description: A variable that stores a score, status, or other result.

declaration_kind.label: Template
declaration_kind.plain_description: A variable used to create item variants.

declaration_kind.label: Context
declaration_kind.plain_description: A global value available to processing rules.

cardinality.label: Multiple
cardinality.plain_description: A list of values where order does not matter.

cardinality.label: Ordered
cardinality.plain_description: A list of values where order matters.

cardinality.label: Record
cardinality.plain_description: A set of named values with their own types.

base_type.label: File
base_type.plain_description: Uploaded file bytes with content type and optional filename.

base_type.label: Pair
base_type.plain_description: Two IDs where direction does not matter.

view.label: Author
view.plain_description: Intended for someone authoring the assessment.

view.label: Candidate
view.plain_description: Intended for the learner or test taker.

view.label: Proctor
view.plain_description: Intended for the person supervising the assessment.

view.label: Scorer
view.plain_description: Intended for someone scoring the assessment.

view.label: Test constructor
view.plain_description: Intended for someone assembling the test.

view.label: Tutor
view.plain_description: Intended for an educator or tutor supporting the learner.

interaction_type.label: Choice
interaction_type.plain_description: Select one or more choices.

interaction_type.label: Ordering
interaction_type.plain_description: Put choices in order.

interaction_type.label: Associate
interaction_type.plain_description: Create pairings between choices.

interaction_type.label: Match
interaction_type.plain_description: Match choices from one set to another.

interaction_type.label: Gap match
interaction_type.plain_description: Put choices into gaps in surrounding content.

interaction_type.label: Text entry
interaction_type.plain_description: Type a short answer inline.

interaction_type.label: Extended text
interaction_type.plain_description: Type an essay or longer response.

interaction_type.label: Inline choice
interaction_type.plain_description: Choose text inside a sentence or passage.

interaction_type.label: Hot text
interaction_type.plain_description: Select highlighted text.

interaction_type.label: Hotspot
interaction_type.plain_description: Click or tap one or more regions on an image.

interaction_type.label: Select point
interaction_type.plain_description: Mark one or more points on an image.

interaction_type.label: Graphic order
interaction_type.plain_description: Put image hotspots into order.

interaction_type.label: Graphic associate
interaction_type.plain_description: Pair image regions with each other.

interaction_type.label: Graphic gap match
interaction_type.plain_description: Match choices to image regions.

interaction_type.label: Position object
interaction_type.plain_description: Place an image object onto a stage.

interaction_type.label: Slider
interaction_type.plain_description: Choose a number on a range.

interaction_type.label: Upload
interaction_type.plain_description: Upload a response file.

interaction_type.label: Drawing
interaction_type.plain_description: Draw or mark up an image or canvas.

interaction_type.label: Media
interaction_type.plain_description: Play or interact with audio or video.

interaction_type.label: End attempt
interaction_type.plain_description: Trigger response processing, often for a hint or action button.

interaction_type.label: Portable custom
interaction_type.plain_description: Use a packaged custom interaction module.

interaction_type.label: Custom
interaction_type.plain_description: Use proprietary custom behavior preserved for compatibility.

navigation_mode.label: Linear
navigation_mode.plain_description: The learner proceeds in order and cannot freely return to earlier items.

navigation_mode.label: Nonlinear
navigation_mode.plain_description: The learner may navigate items in a freer order.

submission_mode.label: Individual
submission_mode.plain_description: Each item is submitted and processed separately.

submission_mode.label: Simultaneous
submission_mode.plain_description: Responses are submitted together at the end of the part.

orientation.label: Horizontal
orientation.plain_description: Laid out left-to-right or along a horizontal axis.

orientation.label: Vertical
orientation.plain_description: Laid out top-to-bottom or along a vertical axis.

text_format.label: Preformatted
text_format.plain_description: Text where spacing and line breaks should be preserved.

text_format.label: XHTML
text_format.plain_description: Structured rich text marked up as XHTML.

shape.label: Ellipse
shape.plain_description: An ellipse hotspot preserved for compatibility.

processing_kind.label: Response processing
processing_kind.plain_description: Rules that calculate item outcomes from learner responses.

processing_kind.label: Outcome processing
processing_kind.plain_description: Rules that calculate test outcomes from item outcomes.

processing_kind.label: Template processing
processing_kind.plain_description: Rules that set template values before delivery.

processing_kind.label: Branch rule
processing_kind.plain_description: Logic that jumps to a target when a condition is true.

processing_kind.label: Precondition
processing_kind.plain_description: Logic that decides whether to skip a part, section, or item.

feedback_kind.label: Modal feedback
feedback_kind.plain_description: Item-level feedback shown based on an outcome value.

feedback_kind.label: Test feedback
feedback_kind.plain_description: Test-level feedback shown during or after the test.

feedback_kind.label: Rubric block
feedback_kind.plain_description: Directions, scoring, navigation, or support information for a selected audience.

show_hide.label: Show
show_hide.plain_description: Hidden by default; show when the condition matches.

show_hide.label: Hide
show_hide.plain_description: Shown by default; hide when the condition matches.

feedback_access.label: During
feedback_access.plain_description: Feedback may appear during the test.

feedback_access.label: At end
feedback_access.plain_description: Feedback appears at the end of the test.

rubric_use.label: Navigation
rubric_use.plain_description: Information about moving around the assessment.

accessibility_support.label: Spoken
accessibility_support.plain_description: Spoken version or content is available.

material_type.label: Calculator
material_type.plain_description: A calculator is allowed or required.

material_type.label: Ruler
material_type.plain_description: A ruler is allowed or required.

material_type.label: Protractor
material_type.plain_description: A protractor is allowed or required.

material_type.label: Digital material
material_type.plain_description: A digital file or material is available.

material_type.label: Physical material
material_type.plain_description: A physical item or material is needed.

alignment_target_type.label: CASE item
alignment_target_type.plain_description: A CASE competency, standard, or skill item.

alignment_target_type.label: URI
alignment_target_type.plain_description: A web URI or external standards target.

alignment_target_type.label: Local
alignment_target_type.plain_description: A local district, publisher, or authoring-system alignment target.

artifact_role.label: Item XML
artifact_role.plain_description: A QTI assessment item XML file.

artifact_role.label: Test XML
artifact_role.plain_description: A QTI assessment test XML file.

artifact_role.label: Media
artifact_role.plain_description: An image, audio, video, or other media file.

artifact_role.label: Stylesheet
artifact_role.plain_description: A CSS or style file used to render content.

unsupported_or_deferred.reason: The platform is first modeling repository, import, search, alignment, and governance. Delivery requires a tested QTI runtime engine.

unsupported_or_deferred.area: Every low-level XML child node as a SQL column
unsupported_or_deferred.reason: The original QTI XML is preserved as the source artifact. Only public platform fields are projected into SQL/API fields.
Processing logic is preserved and summarized. Executing it safely belongs to a QTI runtime service.
CAT depends on mature item metadata, delivery services, and psychometric configuration beyond this repository dictionary.
Vendor extensions are preserved and governed, but not promoted to core platform fields until intentionally modeled.
